During his youth, Gan Ning was known for his mischievous conduct within his community. However, as he matured, he delved into studying the classics and ultimately renounced his previous misdeeds. Gan Ning served as the Minister of Shu Commandery, and later pledged his allegiance to Sun Quan, rising to prominence as a valiant general under his command. Renowned for his bravery and martial prowess, Gan Ning achieved numerous military triumphs throughout his illustrious career. Notably, he launched a daring nighttime assault on Cao Cao’s camp and safeguarded Sun Quan from imminent peril during the pivotal Battle of Xiaoyaojin. Despite his rugged appearance, Gan Ning held scholars in high regard and commanded steadfast loyalty from his soldiers, earning the prestigious title of "The Tiger General of Jiangbiao." Gan Ning's illustrious career showcased his exceptional valor and sagacity.
desc.content2: There does not seem to be much link between his storyline character and his ability to use “Burning Bridges”. However the name of the ability is with reference to his famous night raid on Wei's camps. Yet that raid did not cripple any of Wei's supplies or operations, so even there the link is weak. This ability is one for the game mechanics rather than fidelity to the story.
desc.content3:
There is something about Gan Ning I personally admire, and that is the "I don't give a shit about rules and convention" attitude. As a youngster, he was a pirate, looting and murdering and wreaking havoc. It was because of this background that he was rejected from joining Liu Biao's service. However in his later days, Gan Ning proved himself to be a fearless leader with unconventional tactics. 

His most glorious moment came in his night raid on Wei's camps as a distractionary tactic. After Sun Quan's attack was resisted by Zhang Liao, Cao Cao himself claimed to be bringing a huge reinforcement to back up Zhang Liao. Upon hearing this, the Wu advisor suggested a distractionary raid on Cao Wei's camps. Gan Ning not only volunteered, he brought only 100 men and promised that all 100 would come back alive. In the commando style raid, Gan Ning had his 100 men randomly strike different areas of the camp swiftly and set fire at numerous places. The enemy was disoriented by the scattered flames and did not know where the attack was coming from or how many were attacking. Gan Ning then quickly retreated, all 100 men unharmed, leaving the Cao Wei camp smoldering. It was thus that Sun Quan famously said, "Cao Cao has Zhang Liao while I have Gan Ning".

desc.content1: One of the most prominent generals from the Wu Kingdom, Lv Meng started out as a military general with scant knowledge of tactics and strategy. Following his rising appointments in Wu, Sun Quan himself advised Lv Meng to read in his spare time. Lv Meng wisely did so and his wisdom and knowledge made great leaps in a very short time.
desc.content2:
There appears to be two possible links for "Self-Restraint" to the story. This first is philosophical, where Lv Meng's diligence in his studies is a result of being able to master his time management and overcome his procrastination. He once complained to Sun Quan that he did not have time to read, but Sun Quan aptly replied that himself is busier than Lv Meng yet he can find the time to read.

About "Decisive Planning": Lv Meng crossed a river in white robes for the purpose of deceiving Guan Yu's troops. Lv Meng's troops hid their weapons and pretended to be merchants (who were dressed in white in those days). Only at the last moment did they break their cover and surprise the enemy. This is indeed very similar to Lv Meng's game character! Always undercover and nondescript, but when he explodes he can be extremely lethal.
desc.content3:
Lv Meng was celebrated not only for his bravery but also for his relentless pursuit of knowledge. He inspired a profound saying: "When meeting a man after three days of absence, view him with renewed perspective." In his brief 42 years of life, he made significant contributions to the rise of the Wu Kingdom as a major power. So impressive was his improvement, in fact, that Sun Quan's able advisor Lu Su commented the famous phrase:
"When the clueless Lv Meng is under Wu Kingdom's nurturing, one must re-evaluate him in a different light."

Most famous achievement - Defeating Guan Yu. One of the key reasons for Guan Yu's defeat was Lv Meng's capture of Jing Zhou. Lv Meng subsequently treated the citizens of Jing Zhou extremely well, leading to Guan Yu's troops (mainly recruited from Jing Zhou) to desert Guan Yu and return home. With a depleting army, Guan Yu escaped to Maicheng and was ultimately captured and executed by Sun Quan.

Cause of death - Guan Yu's death did not mark the end of Guan Yu. Instead the spirit of Guan Yu possessed Lv Meng at a celebratory banquet thrown by Sun Quan. At the banquet, the possessed Lv Meng grabbed hold of Sun Quan and swore revenge. Lv Meng then collapsed and died. This story ultimately spawned the God Guan Yu general.

desc.content1: Lu Xun, was a celebrated general of Wu, hailing from the respected Lu family of Wu Commandery in Jiangdong. He was a visionary , excelling in both civil administration and military affairs and assumed significant positions in Sun Quan's government. An idiom that describes him best would be "don't judge a book by it's cover". For the readers unfamiliar with the story, this geeky, nerdy scholar that looks like he cannot survive a papercut is the person who is responsible for the death of Liu Bei. He used his scholarly persona to trick his enemies into letting their guard down. This was used to great effect in Wu’s capture of Jingzhou, which resulted in the death of Guan Yu. Two of the biggest names in the Kingdom of Shu owe their demise to this kid. Geeks rule!
desc.content2:
"Humility/Modesty" describes Lu Xun's careful management of his troops and his unwavering focus on defeating Shu. We suppose that the reason he cannot be targeted for STEAL is to imply the opposite of a careless, arrogant leader. As for ACEDIA, it made sense that he cannot be targeted since Lu Xun's patience and focus on defeating Shu-Han clearly destroys the meaning of that tool.

"Evaluate Situation" describes Lu Xun's victory at the battle of Yiling, where his use of fire to incinerate Liu Bei's forces is compounded by Liu Bei's decision to have all his camps close to each other and within the forested region. 

The “Second Wind” is a short-form of the full phrase which means "the burning of a string of camps connected together for 350km at the battle of Yiling".
desc.content3: Most famous achievement - Defeating Liu Bei at battle of Yiling. After a stalemate, Liu Bei taunted Lu Xun to lure his troops into an ambush. Typical of the humble man that he is, Lu Xun kept his cool and ignored the taunting. Lu Xun knew it was not yet time for the attack. Liu Bei finally gave up and the stalemate continued, but the weather was very hot and dry. Liu Bei made the decision to move all the camps into the forest for some shade. Lu Xun seized the opportunity and set fire to the dry forests. The blaze burned most of Liu Bei's troops and supplies because their escape route was cut off. Liu Bei suffered severe losses and retreated. He never recovered from the loss and subsequently passed on.

name: Zhou Yu
title: Cao Pi
sub: Hegemonic Successor/Successor of the Dominant Empire

desc.content1: The successor to Cao Cao, Cao Pi's ascension to the throne of Wei is fraught with controversy. Cao Cao literally had a litter of male children, all of which were eligible to succession. The most promising among them were Cao Ang, Cao Zhang, Cao Zhi, Cao Chong and Cao Pi. Cao Ang was killed in action early on at the battle at Wancheng. Cao Chong was a child prodigy and reportedly Cao Cao's favorite son. This was where the controversy starts. Cao Chong died mysteriously of an illness at age 13 and it is believed that Cao Pi had something to do with it. After Cao Cao's death, Cao Zhang appeared to be planning to seize power from Cao Pi. Cao Pi promptly sent Cao Zhang back to the frontlines, far from the capital. Cao Pi then crushed many talented staff of Cao Zhi to cripple his power. He even wanted Cao Zhi executed, which led to Cao Zhi's famous "7 step poem". Ultimately, Cao Pi was two extremes of achievements: he dissolved the Han Dynasty became the first Emperor of Wei, yet was quite a failure both domestically and in war.
desc.content2:
"Inheritance/Successor" makes it for interesting discussion. It definitely has link to the story, but it is not clear in which context. Many characters have died prematurely whose deaths are attributed to Cao Pi, even his own wife Lady Zhen. This forms the basis for the game ability to take all the cards, since it implies he was the puppet master behind the scenes and their death is his advantage. However some argue that Cao Pi himself died an "unnaturally early" death at the age of 39.

"Banishment" is significantly mentioned in the story as Cao Pi's preferred punishment for his political opponents. His victims include his (half) brothers Cao Zhi and Cao Zhang, as well as the last Emperor of Han Dynasty.
desc.content3:
Though Cao Cao had this in his sights for years, he decided to let his descendants form the empire and Cao Pi fulfilled that role. In an interesting twist of fate, Sima Yi copied Cao Cao by personally building the foundation of the Jin Dynasty, but allowing his descendants to follow through. It is ironic that the first of the three kingdoms to fall to Sima Yi was Wei.

Cao Pi died of illness at age 39. It is believed that his failure to defeat Wu and Shu led to embarrassment, something which Cao Pi severely resented. Unable to swallow his pride, this unhappiness precipitated into illness which killed him.

desc.content1: The leader and inciter of the yellow turban rebellion, Zhang Jiao became a hero to those suffering under the corrupt and decaying Han Dynasty. Due to a terrible famine that hit China at around 180 AD, the people suffered terribly and yet were further tormented by corrupt and greedy officials. It was then that Zhang Jiao met a strange old man in a cave which gave him 3 books, said to contain the ways of a mysterious art. After mastering the books, Zhang Jiao could control the wind, rain, and lightning and became a sorcerer. Despite having these powers, Zhang Jiao won the hearts of the people by providing aid and supplies to those in need. It was thus that tens of thousands rallied behind Zhang Jiao in his bid to overthrow the Han Dynasty. He never succeeded however, and was defeated by those who were loyal to dynasty, namely Liu Bei and Cao Cao.
desc.content2: In the story, there wasn't much mention of Zhang Jiao himself. From what is written, it is known that can he control the weather. Therefore "Thunder Strike" is a direct link. There is no mention of any form of deception or intervention such as "Devilish Arts/Dark Magic", however.
desc.content3: Most famous achievement - Inciting the Yellow Turban Rebellion and opening the epic Three Kingdoms story. Zhang Jiao appears in Chapter 1 of the story and his valiant efforts in galvanizing the people led to the rebellion. This rebellion is critically important as it marked the beginning of the end of the Han Dynasty, though the rebellion itself fizzled away quickly.

desc.content1:
Zhou Yu, was a distinguished general, statesman, and strategist who served under the leadership of Wu during the waning years of the Eastern Han dynasty in China. Coming from the prestigious Zhou family of Lujiang Commandery, Zhou Yu forged a deep bond with Sun Ce in their formative years. Their enduring friendship blossomed as they joined forces to bring peace and stability to Jiangdong. 

Both a military general and a strategist, he became Sun Quan's able right-hand man and was the brains behind the battle of Red Cliffs 赤壁之战. Unfortunately for him, Zhuge Liang is just too incredibly smart. Despite coming up with schemes to rid Zhuge Liang, Zhou Yu simply cannot outsmart the unrealistically intelligent Zhuge Liang. The story seems to downplay Zhou Yu's achievements and make him seem second class and inferior to Zhuge Liang. However, Zhou Yu's contribution to Cao Cao's defeat at Red Cliffs is incredibly significant and has guaranteed him a place both in the history books and in semi-fictional accounts of the era.
desc.content2: "Convert Spy/Sowing Distrust" is comes from a significant event leading up to the battle at Red Cliffs, where Cao Cao is tricked by Zhou Yu into executing the only 2 generals with experience at naval combat, Cai Mao and Zhang Yun. Unfortunately, that too has nothing to do with how the ability is played in the card game.
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Defeating Cao Cao at Red Cliffs. Zhou Yu used a series of ploys to completely fuddle Cao Cao, all of which ran concurrently. These include getting Pang Tong to trick Cao Cao into shackling his ships together, getting Huang Gai to feint defection so as to set the ships ablaze, making use of Jiang Gan to sow discord between Cao Cao and his navy generals Cai Mao and Zhang Yun.

Cause of death - One of the most famous episodes in the story talks about Zhuge Liang "frustrating Zhou Yu 3 times". Zhou Yu was completely outwitted by Zhuge Liang and loss of troops, Sun Quan's sister Sun Shangxiang, as well as important nodes such as Nanjun and Jingzhou. Zhou Yu literally vomited blood and died of frustration. Historical records, however, showed that Zhou Yu died while preparing for a battle, thus rendering this storyline as drama.
Tragically, he succumbed to illness in Baqiu Commandery while enroute to Shu at the young age of only 36.

desc.content1: One of the early generals before the Three Kingdoms were formed, Taishi Ci was one of Sun Ce's most capable generals. A man with a criminal past, Taishi Ci was denied higher positions and responsibilities by his previous leader Liu Yao. In one instance, Taishi Ci battled Sun Ce and were equally matched. This gave Sun Ce respect for Taishi Ci. When Sun Ce eventually defeated Liu Yao, Sun Ce promised Taishi Ci acknowledgement of his capabilities with important positions. Taishi Ci accepted and thus became a loyal and capable general under Wu till his death.
desc.content2:
Most famous achievement - Rallying his troops to join Sun Ce within 24 hours. When Taishi Ci agreed to join Sun Ce, he asked Sun Ce for one day's absence to convince his troops to continue joining him under Sun Ce's leadership. Sun Ce note only agreed, he even told Taishi Ci to tell his troops that those not willing were free to leave and will not be subjected to martial law. Sun Ce's advisors told him to be wary of Taishi Ci taking the opportunity to escape, but Sun Ce had faith in Taishi Ci's integrity. Indeed, Taishi Ci kept to his word, returning within 24 hours and bringing with him every single one of his men to join Sun Ce.

Cause of death - In the story, Taishi Ci was fatally wounded by an arrow from Wei's Zhang Liao at the battle of Hefei. However, historically Taishi Ci died of illness at age 41.

desc.content1: Wife of Zhou Yu and younger sister of Da Qiao, Xiao Qiao is the starry-eyed girl next door famous for her musical talent. As explained in my post on Da Qiao, the two sisters were one of the major reasons for Wu going to war with Wei, which quickly resulted in the battle of Red Cliffs. Xiao Qiao was notably more vocal and expressive than her aloof elder sister, thus it seemed like Xiao Qiao was the more musically talented of the two, but it has been argued that Da Qiao is equally skilled in music and just chooses to play only for her beau Sun Ce.
desc.content2: Neither of her abilities have any link to the story. Despite her "Heavenly Scent" being horrifyingly powerful, there is just no mention in the story about anything related to her scent doing demonic damage.
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Marrying the brilliant Zhou Yu. It sounds derogatory to say that marrying someone of stature is an "achievement", but she would probably have been viewed upon with envy during her time.

Cause of death - Unknown. Conflicting stories mixed with contaminated legends have churned out an array of different endings for Xiao Qiao. The two most common are "grieving for Zhou Yu till she died of old age" and "dying of illness".

desc.content1: Quite possibly the best fighter in all of China in that era, Lv Bu was a formidable general that struck fear in his opponents. Yet for all his military prowess and combat ability, Lv Bu was not the smartest of people. Throw in a tinge of greed, a sprinkle of lust, and you have a riding, fighting disaster atop four hooves. Dong Zhuo found that out the hard way. To win Lv Bu's favour, Dong Zhuo showered Lv Bu with expensive gifts such as the Red Hare horse and an assortment of jewels. Lv Bu came under Dong Zhuo's wing after all receiving all those goodies, then ended up murdering Dong Zhuo victim over a woman. After Dong Zhuo's death, Lv Bu became a minor warlord and led a series of battles in an effort to expand his territory. However his poor use of strategy and refusal to listen to advice ultimately caused his downfall.
desc.content2:
The link can actually be found in the subscript line written under his ability description. It is a phrase said by Chen Gong, his advisor when Lv Bu became warlord, that reads:
"His courage and bravery, unsurmountable. His eagerness for battle, unprecedented. Yet brave but without cunning. Violent but without benevolence."

"Unparalleled/Unrivaled" and refers to his incredibly combat skills. Thankfully, it does not seem to be sarcastic referring to his intellect as well.
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Killing Dong Zhuo. Ok so maybe it's not a very glamorous view of Lu Bu to say it was his "most famous achievement" when he was merely a pawn of Wang Yun's scheme, but the fellow did kill the tyrant and the world celebrated Dong Zhuo's death. Coming in second would be his securing a truce between Yuan Shu and Liu Bei's forces by showing off his archery skills, but that seemed somewhat less spectacular in comparison, doesn't it?

In the days of Yuan Shao's coalition against Dong Zhuo, many generals challenged Lv Bu, not none were his match. One of these generals was Gongsun Zan, who could hardly hold up a dozen bouts against the mighty Lv Bu. Out came Zhang Fei who raced out to meet Lv Bu, thus saving Gongsun Zan. The two were evenly matched when Guan Yu joined in. 1 VS 2 and still Lv Bu held up strong. Liu Bei joined in the fray and it became 3 of the legendary heroes (all highly capable fighters) against 1 single Lv Bu. Though Lv Bu could match them 1 VS 3, alas he became fatigued and he retreated. Still, the entire coalition of thousands was watching the duel and everyone had little doubt of the fighting prowess of Lv Bu.

Cause of death - Betrayed by his own men to Cao Cao and executed. For all his fighting valour, Lv Bu became a coward in the face of death. When Lv Bu was under siege, he chose to stay by the side of his wives and not listen to his advisor, Chen Gong. He was also ill-tempered to his men, their low-morale fueling resentment for their leader. While Lv Bu was asleep, they stole the Sky Piercing Halberd and bundled Lv Bu over to Cao Cao. Lv Bu asked Cao Cao to allow him to surrender and he would be at Cao Cao's service. However Liu Bei quickly reminded Cao Cao of the fate of Lv Bu's previous masters, and Cao Cao ordered Lv Bu executed without hesitation. Lv Bu pleaded for his life to no avail. In sharp contrast, Lv Bu's general Zhang Liao cursed Cao Cao despite being bound for certain death. Cao Cao unbound Zhang Liao, and Zhang Liao then became one of Cao Cao's most able general in the years to come.

desc.content1: An extremely beautiful woman with smarts and capability, Lady Zhen was Cao Pi's first wife. The word Ji is a title for "lady". Thus her real name is not Zhen Ji, but rather Zhen Luo. Her life was a down-hill rollercoaster of misfortune and victimization. She lost her father at the age of 2, was married to Yuan Shao's son Yuan Xi, but was forcefully remarried to Cao Pi after he became obsessed with her beauty. After giving birth to Cao Rui, she fell out of favor with Cao Pi due to rumors and bad-mouthing from his concubine, Guo Nv Wang. Lady Zhen never did manage to win back Cao Pi's favor. Instead, the bad-mouthing from Guo Nv Wang became worse. Lady Zhen paid the ultimate price when Cao Pi, poisoned by Guo Nv Wang's words, forced Lady Zhen to commit suicide.
desc.content2: "Empress Dowager" is not linked to the story. Though we know that Cao Pi peeled her from Yuan Xi, she was not the reason Cao Cao destroyed Yuan Shao and family. This ability is probably centered around game mechanics than history. "Goddess of Luo River" stems from a poem written by Cao Zhi, Cao Pi's brother, known as "Goddess of the Luo River", apparently praising the goddess for her ethereal beauty, intelligence and capability. It is rumored that Cao Zhi had an affair with Lady Zhen.
desc.content3:
Giving birth to the man who would become Emperor of Wei, Cao Rui. This became the sore point that eventually led to her death, because Guo Nu Wang poisoned Cao Pi into believing that Cao Rui is not his child but Yuan Xi's.

Cause of death - Suicide... but more likely forced than willing. The exact method of death is unclear, but one story goes that Cao Pi personally handed her a glass of poison to drink. Lady Zhen knows full well what it means, so after drinking but before the poison takes effect, she cleans the glass. This is so as to protect Cao Pi's reputation, since for an emperor to murder his own wife is detrimental to his reign.

desc.content1: Regarded as one of the 4 great beauties of ancient China, Diao Chan is one of the classic examples of the "Sow Discord". The willing pawn of Wang Yun in his grand scheme to destroy the tyrannical Dong Zhuo, Diao Chan flirted with Lv Bu while submitting herself to Dong Zhuo as his concubine. In doing so, she pitted godfather against godson, causing severe dissension and distrust amongst them. Dong Zhuo relents that he needs the incredible combat skills of Lv Bu to maintain his power, but Lv Bu had little to lose. Wang Yun further convinces Lv Bu to murder Dong Zhuo and take Diao Chan for himself, which he eventually does. In some versions of the story, Diao Chan is said to have truly loved Lv Bu but became distraught on knowing Wang Yun's scheme. The original version, however, seems to state that Diao Chan knowingly wanted to kill Dong Zhuo and participated actively in Wang Yun's ploy.
desc.content2: "Sow Discord/Lust" is one of the best matched ability to the story in the entire game. This ability completely embodies Diao Chan's persona in the story, which is simply using her beauty to cause two men to fight each other. "Beauty Outshining the Moon" comes from a longer proverb used to describe the 4 great beauties of ancient China, which means the fish stare at them till they forget to swim and sink to the bottom, the geese forget to flap their wings and fall to earth, the moon hides behind the clouds in envy, and the flowers slink low and bow the petals in shame.
desc.content3:
She caused Lv Bu to kill Dong Zhuo and, thus, ridding the world of a super tyrant. To be blunt, Diao Chan is hardly known for anything else other than this achievement and her beauty.

Many versions abound with regards to the fate of Diao Chan after Dong Zhuo's death. Some say she was killed while Lv Bu escaped, some said she died with Lv Bu after he was executed by Cao Cao. More extreme versions claim she was presented as a gift to Guan Yu from Cao Cao for his temporary allegiance to the Wei Kingdom.

name: Lu Su
title: Cai Wenji
sub: Estranged Orphan/Girl in a Strange Land

desc.content1: Like a scene out of a Chinese horror movie, a lone damsel sits in the night out in the woods, strumming an ancient Chinese string instrument. Her long straight hair gently swaying along with the willows, her sharp face pale, white and mournful. Who is Lady Cai? What is her role in the story? Truth be told, the name Cai Wenji was complete new to me when I heard about this character in the Hills Pack. As it turns out, she was a very minor character, a musician and poet, but with a sad life story. Married at the age of 15 but widowed soon after, she was then held captive by the Xiong Nu clan chieftain for many years. He then made her his wife and she bore him children, but her heart had always yearned for home. It would be 12 years later before a particular famous warlord ransomed her... but only to fulfill his own agenda. This warlord would go on to become one of the most famous person throughout Chinese history. Who was this warlord? Read on the find out!
desc.content2: The ability mechanics do not have a direct link to the story, but the names of the abilities do. Both "Broken Heart" and "Song of Sorrow" relates to the life of Lady Cai. After being imprisoned by the Xiongnu nomads, she composed a series of famous musical pieces known as the "Eighteen Songs of a Nomad Flute". Later in her life, she wrote some poems with a sorrowful tone, lamenting her turbulent life. See? What did I tell you? The perfect character for a sorrowful female apparition!
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Composing poems and pieces of music. Lady Cai was a talented poet and musician. After her unfortunate capture by Xiongnu nomads, she composed "Eighteen Songs of a Nomad Flute", a sorrowful song in the style of nomadic tunes.

It was 12 years later that she was ransomed Cao Cao, yet Cao Cao did so for his own objectives. She was to become a pacifier to the spirits for her clan, as by that time Lady Cai was the last surviving member. Even then, Lady demonstrated her memory and was able to recite about four hundred of her father’s lost works of poem and songs.

Cause of death - Unknown. There has been no record of her death, but her legacy lives on. Almost 500 years later, a series of paintings were commissioned by the first emperor of the Song Dynasty called "Eighteen Songs of a Nomad Flute". It is said that some fragments of these 18 paintings survive till this day in Boston Museum of Fine Arts.

desc.content1: At one point of time he became the most powerful warlord in all of China, thanks to a coalition of numerous warlords in an effort to overthrow the tyrannical Dong Zhuo. He was the most powerful because he was nominated to lead the entire coalition, akin to the modern day Secretary-General of the United Nations, thanks to his noble ancestry. His great-grandfather was an interior minister that served directly under the Emperor. However Yuan Shao's power was short-lived, as he very quickly lost the respect of the other warlords in the coalition. He became known as an incompetent leader that made poor use of his resources and was too indecisive to be effective. The coalition fell apart and Yuan Shao turned back to being warlord of the northern territories. He did not last very long as Cao Cao sought to eliminate him and bring the entire northern China under his rule.
desc.content2: Warlord Yuan Shao and his choice of using arrows heavily. In his attacks against Gongsun Zan and Cao Cao, there is an abnormally large number of mentions of Yuan Shao using arrows against his enemies. This is likely the reason Yuan Shao's character in this card game has the ability to use “Raining Arrows” easily.
desc.content3:
Leading the coalition of forces against Dong Zhuo. There were at least 10 different warlords in the coalition with troops numbering half a million or more. Even before Dong Zhuo took the Emperor hostage, Yuan Shao was adamant in killing off the corrupt officials that caused the decay of the dynasty. In a way, Yuan Shao is not a bad guy: he just does not make a very effective leader.

Cao Cao defeated Yuan Shao at the battle of Guandu, which became highlighted as a prime show of Cao Cao's superior strategy and tactics. Yuan Shao had a force many times larger than Cao Cao, but yet he suffered a terrible loss. Cao Cao analyzed Yuan Shao as being unwilling to listen to his advisors, stubborn, and tolerating general disunity under his rule. Yuan Shao did not die in battle, but he did not live long after either. As a show of respect for his former friend, Cao Cao paid his respects at Yuan Shao's tomb later on.

desc.content1: The top 2 military generals under Yuan Shao, Yan Liang and Wen Chou were good friends and were said to be equal in combat skills. The story did not give them much air time, but enough mention was made to highlight their ability. Under the early coalition led by Yuan Shao, a formidable opponent called Hua Xiong challenged the coalition and killed many generals. Yuan Shao lamented that both Yan Liang and Wen Chou were not around, else they would surely destroy Hua Xiong easily. Later parts of the story did not mention both Yan Liang and Wen Chou together in combat, but these two are typically associated. It does seem like their capabilities were over-promised as both fell victim to Guan Yu.
desc.content2: Very little relation can be drawn from the story linking Duel to these two characters. All that is known is that both of them are commonly referred together as a pair, thus the name "Dual Heroes".
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Neither of them had much notable achievement, except their leader Yuan Shao's praise for them.

Both were killed by Guan Yu in battle. Yan Liang had his head severed by Guan Yu and brought back to Cao Cao during the battle at Baima. Guan Yu was virtually unopposed as he charged straight towards Yan Liang. With one blow he struck down Yan Liang, then severed his head and brought it back by tying it around his horse’s neck. Wen Chou was killed in a subsequent battle. His troops fell into disorder after Cao Cao cunningly left numerous horses for Wen Chou's troops to plunder. Cao Cao's generals attacked in the midst of the disorder and Wen Chou was forced to flee. However, Guan Yu was waiting at his retreat route and they fought a few rounds. Wen Chou retreated hastily, but Guan Yu was riding the Red Hare of Lv Bu, whose speed allowed him to catch up to Wen Chou and slay him from behind.

desc.content1: The second smartest person of the era, probably also the ugliest in looks. Pang Tong is supposedly Zhuge Liang's equal in the brains department. Where Zhuge Liang is given the nickname "Hidden Dragon", Pang Tong is given the nickname "Rising Phoenix". However his hideous looks made some doubt his ability, such as Sun Quan. Pang Tong is credited as the critical link behind Cao Cao's spectacular defeat at battle of Red Cliffs, however, he did not live very long to share his talents. Imagine how the world would have been different had Pang Tong lived a decade longer, where both he and Zhuge Liang were helping Liu Bei.
desc.content2:
"Chain Stratagem" is a perfect match for Pang Tong since he practically masterminded the shackling of Cao Cao's ships at the battle of Red Cliffs. The shackles turned out to be the key reason for the blaze which wiped out Cao Cao's troops. 

"Nirvana" is an interesting skill. Players have commented that the game makers are making up for Pang Tong's early death by allowing him to resurrect in the card game. Then again, a lot of other characters died early but the game makers never gave them resurrection abilities. I mean look at Guo Jia, he was an exceptional advisor and died young. No second-life for him in this game!
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Tricking Cao Cao into shackling all his ships together prior to the battle of Red Cliffs, such that when Zhou Yu set the ships ablaze, there was no escape! Pang Tong is also credited as the main motivator behind Liu Bei's occupation of the west, which became the stronghold for Shu.

Cause of death - Pang Tong was ambushed by arrows as he led troops to attack Luocheng. Liu Bei decided to send Pang Tong by the shortcut, where ambushes were to be expected, even though Liu Bei himself was the combat personnel and Pang Tong was an advisor. The location of Pang Tong's death is called Luofengpo, which translates to Valley of the fallen phoenix. How apt.

desc.content1: Likely one of the most despised characters in the entire era, Liu Shan is the son and successor of Liu Bei. He took over the reins of the Shu Kingdom from an early age of 16 (the year that Liu Bei died), and is infamous for his inability as a ruler... or at least that is what the story wants us to think. There was a huge chunk of history missing between the years Liu Shan first ruled Shu till the fall of the kingdom 40 years later. In the absence of recorded facts, myths and rumors have not been kind to Liu Shan and painted a picture of an indulgent and indecisive ruler. Ultimately, the kingdom of Shu so painstakingly created by Liu Bei, Zhuge Liang and gang, were all destroyed during Liu Shan's reign. Legend has it that Liu Shan's intellect was compromised when his own father threw the baby Liu Shan on the ground! Why would Liu Bei do such a thing? Read on to find out!
desc.content2:
"Hedonism/Relish" refers to Liu Shan’s lifestyle. Liu Shan was interested in playing and having fun, but never in politics. He continued to squander his material wealth and abundance even after the downfall of Shu. However, some say his indulgent lifestyle was all just a ploy to keep him away from danger. Even though he was captured by Wei forces, he was given nobility in Wei's courts and allowed to enjoy his final years in peace. The mechanics of this ability cleverly shields Liu Shan from ATTACKS, allowing him (you) to stay safe and happy.

"Delegation" refers to his political strategies. He allowed his trusted officials to handle all state affairs, regardless of their capability. When he gave the power to wise people like Zhuge Liang and Jiang Wei, the Shu kingdom was efficient and strong. However, it proved to be a mistake to give such powers to Huang Hao, the corrupt and incapable eunuch who Liu Shan trusted. The Shu kingdom quickly became chaotic, and ultimately crumbled. The game mechanics allows Liu Shan to "delegate" his action phase, granting an extra turn to another player that you trust. Don't make the same mistake as Liu Shan! Watch out for the defectors!
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Losing his "brain cells" when Liu Bei tossed him. Ok perhaps that's not exactly an achievement, but it's still very significant in popular folklore. At the battle of Changban, Zhao Yun risked life and limb to rescue the baby Liu Shan from Wei forces. When Liu Bei saw the wounds that his beloved general suffered to save his son, Liu Bei tossed his baby to the ground and proclaimed "To preserve that suckling I very nearly lost a great general!" It is said that Liu Shan suffered a concussion as a result of Liu Bei's over-drama, and hence became "stupid" for the rest of his life.

Cause of death - Old age. After living in captivity in Wei, Liu Shan never did think much about his own kingdom that he lost. All he seemed to care about was living a carefree life and having ample play-time. Thus Liu Shan was never again regarded as a threat and he was left to while away his remaining 8 years of his life under soft-captivity. He was aptly given the title "The Duke of Peace and Comfort". Because of this, he has become almost synonymous with the sin of Acedia. He was aged 64 when he passed on.

name: Gongsun Zan
title: Ma Chao
sub: One against a Thousand/A Thousand Cavalries

desc.content1: The valiant general known for his horsemanship and combat ability, Ma Chao is one of Shu's 5 greatest generals. Typically depicted riding a horse, he initially led rebellions against Cao Cao before eventually finding his place under Liu Bei. Ma Chao was highly regarded by Zhuge Liang as an all-rounded civil and military leader.
desc.content2: His "Horse Riding" is a given. Both he and Pang De are known for their skilled horsemanship from the moment they appeared in the story. "Horse Riding", however, is just an added skill by us so as to tackle the "dodgy" characters, such as Zhang Jiao, Younger Zhuge Liang and Lady Zhen.
desc.content3:
Most famous achievement - Probably the uprising he led against Cao Cao, where he very nearly captured Cao Cao alive. Cao Cao retaliated by executing over a hundred of Ma Chao's family and extended family members. Though the uprising gave him prominence and respect from the kingdom of Shu Han, the death of hundreds of his family members due to his uprising would haunt him and disgrace him. It even led to an aborted marriage.

Cause of death - Ma Chao succumbed to illness and died. In the story version, Liu Bei dies before Ma Chao, however it seems the actual historical version of events was Ma Chao died earlier and even wrote a parting letter to Liu Bei.

desc.content1: Quite possibly the oldest general by age in Romance of the Three Kingdoms. By the time of his fatal injury that he sustained in combat, Huang Zhong was already 75 years old. His age is probably exaggerated, much like many of the other details in the story. In fact, no record exists that could determine his age. The legend of Huang Zhong is, therefore, much influenced by Romance of the Three Kingdoms and not so much by true history. Nonetheless, Huang Zhong was an incredible general, very powerful in combat and deadly accurate with the bow and arrow. We put his attacking abilities to the extreme! More about his character abilities below.
desc.content3:
Although his biggest credit was killing the Wei  general Xia Houyuan at the battle of Mount Dingjun.

And his chivalry when Guan Yu battled him in their first meet. At that time, Huang Zhong was defending the city of Changsha from the forces of Liu Bei. Guan Yu was sent to duel with Huang Zhong, both of them ferocious but chivalrous. Huang Zhong purposely shot an arrow that hit Guan Yu's helmet instead of killing him, for he had grown to respect Guan Yu very quickly. Guan Yu killed Huang Zhong's horse in battle, but instead of taking advantage, Guan Yu let Huang Zhong rest and find another suitable horse to do battle again. The next day, Huang Zhong repeatedly took shots at Guan Yu with his bow, but did not load any arrows, as a subtle way of thanking Guan Yu. Eventually, Huang Zhong surrendered to Liu Bei after being rescued by Wei Yan after Han Xuan ordered Huang Zhong executed for disloyalty.
desc.content4: Kirin Bow is Huang Zhong's weapon and it is quite obvious what a huge advantage this weapon gives him.  Kirin Bow has an attacking range of 5, which means when Huang Zhong equips this weapon, your opponent needs to have at least 6 cards on-hand to be able to DODGE your ATTACK! Very few characters can hold that many cards outside of their turn! Maybe Lv Meng or Cao Ren at the moment?

name: Hua Xiong
title: Lady Gan
sub: Empress of Shu/Empress of Zhao Lie

desc.content1: Lady Gan is the wife of Liu Bei, the virtuous caretaker of Liu Bei's family affairs, and the biological mother of Liu Shan. She accompanied Liu Bei during his tumultuous early career and often took charge of managing the family affairs in his absence. Lady Gan bore Liu Shan in Jingzhou. Amidst the chaos of the Battle of Changbanpo, she and her son Liu Shan found refuge under the vigilant protection of Zhao Yun. Following the establishment of the state of Shu Han by Liu Bei in the inaugural year of the Zhangwu era (221 AD) and after his ascension as its first emperor, he bestowed the title of "Lady Huangsi" upon Lady Gan in recognition of her contributions after her death. After Liu Shan's ascension to the throne following Liu Bei's passing, he bestowed upon his own mother the title of "Empress of Zhao Lie" and relocated his parents' tombs to Huiling, where they were laid to rest together.
desc.content2:
“Prudence” came from Zhuge Liang’s speech about her. This ability was similar to the beneficial part of Fa Zheng’s “Reciprocation” before change. It is likely referring to the fact that Lady Gan was saved frequently, and she would repay her allies by letting them to draw a card.

“Divine Wisdom” came from a folktale about Lady Gan. It was said that the skin of Lady Gan was pale and clear as jade. When Liu Bei acquired a jade figure, he put it in the room of Lady Gan and compared it to Lady Gan’s skin. However, Lady Gan advised Liu Bei to concentrate in his aspiration, instead of indulging in playthings. As a result, she was praised by the ministers of Shu Kingdom as "The Lady of Divine Wisdom".
desc.content3:
When Liu Bei resided in Xiaopei, he married Lady Gan, who, like Lady Mi, was Liu Bei's wife.
After that, Liu Bei was defeated and joined Yuan Shao. Guan Yu temporarily surrendered to Cao Cao to protect Liu Bei’s two wives. When Guan Yu learned of Liu Bei's whereabouts, he took the risk of taking Lady Gan and Lady Mi to join Liu Bei.
Later on, Lady Gan accompanied Liu Bei to Jingzhou. Shortly after giving birth to Liu Shan, Cao Cao's army raided Jingzhou. Liu Bei fled, Lady Gan and Liu Shan went missing, but was subsequently rescued by Zhao Yun.

Soon after the Battle of the Red Cliff concluded, Lady Gan died. When Liu Bei was defeated, he mentioned that the woman he missed most was Lady Gan.

After Zhou Yu learned about that, he discussed and set up a plot with Sun Quan, using Sun Shang Xiang to marry Liu Bei.
Lady Gan's death also had a great impact on her son Liu Shan.

desc.content1:
Always the general of Shu that appears to save the day when their need is crucial, Zhao Yun is arguably one of the most well-liked characters in the entire story. 

He also was a prominent general of Shu during the Three Kingdoms period. Zhao Yun initially served under the warlord Gongsun Zan, then joined Liu Bei and achieved many remarkable military achievements. Zhao Yun's exceptional military prowess, unwavering loyalty, and remarkable bravery were widely celebrated. His pivotal involvement in the Battle of Changbanpo solidified his reputation as the "embodiment of valor." During this crucial battle, Zhao Yun fearlessly breached the enemy lines seven times to rescue Liu Bei's infant son. Taking into account the broader context, Zhao Yun counseled Liu Bei to prioritize the welfare of the people by restoring their lands and homes. Furthermore, he warned against initiating an assault on Wu, acknowledging the possible dangers and repercussions that could arise from such a military endeavor. Beaten by no one but sickness, Zhao Yun was honored with the title "Marquis Shunping," and his image as an "ever-victorious general" continued to inspire and captivate future generations long after his passing.
desc.content2:
If you expect this general to have abilities to be feared in the card game, then you are right.
Zhao Yun's "Dragon’s Valor" is he has a much stronger evasive ability than most players. Compared to Guan Yu and Zhang Fei who can heap loads of attack damage, Zhao Yun has an abnormal amount of ATTACK cards at his disposal but also has an incredible amount of DODGE as well! You kind of get the impression that we want you to think that Guan Yu and Zhang Fei are pure brute strength while Zhao Yun has more dexterity and flexibility.
desc.content3:
Zhao Yun's rescue of Liu Shan at the Battle of Changban. This story is the legend. When Zhao Yun is separated from Liu Bei in the chaos after Cao Cao attacks, Liu Bei's generals reported seeing Zhao Yun dash in the direction of the enemy alone, inferring that Zhao Yun has deserted Liu Bei and joined the enemy. Liu Bei had none of it and believed Zhao Yun must have some plan up his sleeve. True enough, Zhao Yun ran into the enemy to search for Liu Bei's wife and son, Liu Shan, who was missing as well. After finding Liu Shan but not being able to rescue Liu Bei's wife, Zhao Yun dashes back towards Liu Bei, encountering literally thousands of enemy troops alone. One versus a few thousand, Zhao Yun manages to slay over 50 of Cao Cao's generals and hundreds of troops before successfully escaping. When Liu Shan was finally presented back to Liu Bei, Liu Bei tosses the baby on the ground, exclaiming that his son's life was not worth endangering his great general.

Zhao Yun died of old age, which was a remarkable feat not to have died in battle in that era. He remained undefeated in battle till his death. Most of the other Shu-Han generals were all killed in action.
